The new configuration\n56\t    ///\n57\t    /// # Returns\n58\t    /// The parser with the new configuration\n59\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n60\t    where\n61\t        Self: Sized;\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n65\t///\n66\t/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n67\t/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n68\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n69\tpub struct BasicParser {\n70\t    config: ParserConfig,\n71\t    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n72\t    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n73\t}\n74\t\n75\timpl BasicParser {\n76\t    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n77\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n78\t        Self {\n79\t            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n80\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n81\t        }\n82\t    }\n83\t\n84\t    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n85\t    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n86\t        Self {\n87\t            config,\n88\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    /// Create a new strict parser\n93\t    ///\n94\t    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n95\t    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n96\t    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n97\t        Self {\n98\t            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n99\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n100\t        }\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n104\t    ///\n105\t    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n106\t    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n107\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n108\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n109\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n110\t\n111\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n112\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n113\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n114\t\n115\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n116\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n117\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n118\t                {\n119\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n120\t                }\n121\t                scope_stack.reset();\n122\t                continue;\n123\t            }\n124\t\n125\t            // Skip YAML directives\n126\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n127\t                continue;\n128\t            }\n129\t\n130\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n131\t\n132\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n133\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n134\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n135\t            if indent_changed {\n136\t                // Check if this line has a key context\n137\t                let has_key = extract_key_context(line).is_some();\n138\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n139\t            }\n140\t\n141\t            // Handle blank lines and comments\n142\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n143\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n144\t            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n145\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n146\t                // Process indent transitions without keys (e.g., scope exit on blank line)\n147\t                if indent_changed && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n148\t                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n149\t                }\n150\t                continue;\n151\t            }\n152\t\n153\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n154\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n155\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n156\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n157\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n158\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n159\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n160\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n161\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n162\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n163\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n164\t                                    dup_err.message()\n165\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n166\t                            }\n167\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n168\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n169\t                            {\n170\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n171\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n172\t                            }\n173\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n174\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n175\t                                line_num_1index,\n176\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n177\t                            );\n178\t                        } else {\n179\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n180\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n181\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n182\t                            {\n183\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n184\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n185\t                            }\n186\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n187\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n188\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n189\t                                    dup_err.message()\n190\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n191\t                            }\n192\t                        }\n193\t                    } else {\n194\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n195\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n196\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n197\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n198\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n199\t                        {\n200\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n201\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n202\t                        }\n203\t                    }\n204\t                }\n205\t                Ordering::Less => {\n206\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n207\t                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n208\t                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n209\t                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n210\t                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n211\t                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n212\t\n213\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n214\t                    {\n215\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n216\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n217\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n218\t                    }\n219\t\n220\t                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n221\t                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n222\t                    }\n223\t\n224\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n225\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n226\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n227\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n228\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n229\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n230\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n231\t                                    dup_err.message()\n232\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n233\t                            }\n234\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n235\t                            {\n236\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n237\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n238\t                            }\n239\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n240\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n241\t                                line_num_1index,\n242\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n243\t                            );\n244\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n245\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n246\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n247\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n248\t                                    dup_err.message()\n249\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n250\t                            }\n251\t                        }\n252\t                    }\n253\t                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n254\t                }\n255\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n256\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n257\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n258\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n259\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n260\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n261\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n262\t                            {\n263\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n264\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n265\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n266\t                            }\n267\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n268\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n269\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n270\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n271\t                                    dup_err.message()\n272\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n273\t                            }\n274\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n275\t                            {\n276\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n277\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n278\t                            }\n279\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n280\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n281\t                                line_num_1index,\n282\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n283\t                            );\n284\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n285\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n286\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n287\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n288\t                                    dup_err.message()\n289\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n290\t                            }\n291\t                        }\n292\t                    }\n293\t                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n294\t                }\n295\t            }\n296\t\n297\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n298\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n299\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n300\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n301\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n302\t                    {\n303\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n304\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n305\t                    }\n306\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n307\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n308\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n309\t                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n310\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n311\t                            dup_err.message()\n312\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n313\t                    }\n314\t                } else {\n315\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n316\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n317\t                    {\n318\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n319\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n320\t                    }\n321\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n322\t                }\n323\t            }\n324\t        }\n325\t\n326\t        duplicate_errors\n327\t    }\n328\t}\n329\t\n330\timpl Default for BasicParser {\n331\t    fn default() -> Self {\n332\t        Self::new()\n333\t    }\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\timpl Parser for BasicParser {\n337\t    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n338\t        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n339\t        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n340\t        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n341\t\n342\t        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n343\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n344\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n345\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n346\t\n347\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n348\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n349\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n350\t                {\n351\t                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n352\t                }\n353\t                scope_stack.reset();\n354\t                continue;\n355\t            }\n356\t\n357\t            // Skip YAML directives\n358\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n359\t                continue;\n360\t            }\n361\t\n362\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n363\t\n364\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n365\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n366\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n367\t            if indent_changed {\n368\t                // Check if this line has a key context\n369\t                let has_key = extract_key_context(line).is_some();\n370\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n371\t            }\n372\t\n373\t            // Handle blank lines and comments\n374\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n375\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n376\t            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n377\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n378\t                // Process indent transitions without keys (e.g., scope exit on blank line)\n379\t                if indent_changed && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n380\t                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n381\t                }\n382\t                continue;\n383\t            }\n384\t\n385\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n386\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n387\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n388\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n389\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n390\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n391\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n392\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n393\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n394\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n395\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n396\t                                    dup_err.message()\n397\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n398\t                            }\n399\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n400\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n401\t                            {\n402\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n403\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n404\t                            }\n405\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n406\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n407\t                                line_num_1index,\n408\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n409\t                            );\n410\t                        } else {\n411\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n412\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n413\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n414\t                            {\n415\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n416\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n417\t                            }\n418\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n419\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n420\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n421\t                                    dup_err.message()\n422\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n423\t                            }\n424\t                        }\n425\t                    } else {\n426\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n427\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n428\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n429\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n430\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n431\t                        {\n432\t                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n433\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n434\t                        }\n435\t                    }\n436\t                }\n437\t                Ordering::Less => {\n438\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n439\t                    // Record the indent transition with key\n440\t                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, true, line);\n441\t\n442\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n443\t                    {\n444\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n445\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n446\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n447\t                    }\n448\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n449\t\n450\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n451\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n452\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n453\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n454\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n455\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n456\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n457\t                                    dup_err.message()\n458\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n459\t                            }\n460\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n461\t                            {\n462\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n463\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n464\t                            }\n465\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n466\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n467\t                                line_num_1index,\n468\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n469\t                            );\n470\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n471\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n472\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n473\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n474\t                                    dup_err.message()\n475\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n476\t                            }\n477\t                        }\n478\t                    }\n479\t                }\n480\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n481\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n482\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n483\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n484\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n485\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n486\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n487\t                            {\n488\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n489\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n490\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n491\t                            }\n492\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n493\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n494\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n495\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n496\t                                    dup_err.message()\n497\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n498\t                            }\n499\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n500\t                            {\n501\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n502\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n503\t                            }\n504\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n505\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n506\t                                line_num_1index,\n507\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n508\t                            );\n509\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n510\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n511\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n512\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n513\t                                    dup_err.message()\n514\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n515\t                            }\n516\t                        }\n517\t                    }\n518\t                }\n519\t            }\n520\t\n521\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n522\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n523\t                // When starting a new sequence item, exit any existing sequence scope at the same indent\n524\t                if scope_stack.current_indent() == indent && scope_stack.in_sequence_context() {\n525\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n526\t                    {\n527\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Exiting previous sequence scope before entering new one at indent={}, line={}\",\n528\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n529\t                    }\n530\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent.saturating_sub(1));\n531\t                }\n532\t\n533\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n534\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n535\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n536\t                    {\n537\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n538\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n539\t                    }\n540\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n541\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n542\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n543\t                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n544\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n545\t                            dup_err.message()\n546\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n547\t                    }\n548\t                } else {\n549\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n550\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n551\t                    {\n552\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n553\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n554\t                    }\n555\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n556\t                }\n557\t            }\n558\t        }\n559\t\n560\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n561\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n562\t            Ok(value) => {\n563\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n564\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n565\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n566\t            }\n567\t            Err(err) => {\n568\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n569\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n570\t                    err.to_string()\n571\t                } else {\n572\t                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n573\t                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n574\t                };\n575\t                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n576\t            }\n577\t        }\n578\t    }\n579\t\n580\t    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n581\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n582\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n583\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n584\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n585\t        }\n586\t    }\n587\t\n588\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n589\t        // Read file content\n590\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n591\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n592\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n593\t        }\n594\t    }\n595\t\n596\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n597\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n598\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n599\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n600\t        } else {\n601\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n602\t        };\n603\t\n604\t        // Run syntax validation\n605\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n606\t\n607\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n608\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n609\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n610\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n611\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n612\t                result.valid = false;\n613\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n614\t            }\n615\t        }\n616\t\n617\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n618\t        if result.is_valid() {\n619\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n620\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n621\t\n622\t            // Merge errors from detector\n623\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n624\t                result.valid = false;\n625\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n626\t            }\n627\t        }\n628\t\n629\t        result\n630\t    }\n631\t\n632\t    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n633\t        // Read file content\n634\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n635\t            Ok(content) => content,\n636\t            Err(err) => {\n637\t                return ValidationResult {\n638\t                    valid: false,\n639\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n640\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n641\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n642\t                    )],\n643\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n644\t                };\n645\t            }\n646\t        };\n647\t\n648\t        // Validate the content\n649\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n650\t    }\n651\t\n652\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n653\t        &self.config\n654\t    }\n655\t\n656\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n657\t    where\n658\t        Self: Sized,\n659\t    {\n660\t        Self {\n661\t            config,\n662\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n663\t        }\n664\t    }\n665\t}\n666\t\n667\t// ============================================================================\n668\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n669\t// ============================================================================\n670\t\n671\t#[cfg(test)]\n672\tmod integration_tests {\n673\t    use super::*;\n674\t\n675\t    /// Test parse_str with nested YAML structures\n676\t    #[test]\n677\t    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n678\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n679\t        let yaml = r#\"\n680\tservices:\n681\t  web:\n682\t    host: localhost\n683\t    port: 8080\n684\t  database:\n685\t    host: db.example.com\n686\t    port: 5432\n687\t\"#;\n688\t\n689\t        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n690\t        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse valid nested YAML successfully\");\n691\t\n692\t        // Verify we can access the parsed structure\n693\t        let value = result.unwrap();\n694\t        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n695\t        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n696\t    }\n697\t\n698\t    /// Test parse_str with deeply nested YAML\n699\t    #[test]\n700\t    fn {\n754\t        let parser = BasicParser::strict(); // Strict parser doesn't allow duplicates\n755\t\n756\t        let yaml = r#\"\n757\tconfig:\n758\t  server:\n759\t    host: localhost\n760\t    /// Test parse_str with empty lines and comments\n869\t    #[test]\n870\t    fn test_parse_str_with_comments_and_empty_lines() {\n871\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n872\t\n873\t        let yaml = r#\"\n874\t# Configuration file\n875\tname: test\n876\t\n877\t# Server settings\n878\tserver:\n879\t  host: localhost\n880\t  # Port number\n881\t  port: 8080\n882\t\n883\t# Database     let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n897\t\n898\t        let yaml = r#\"\n899\tconfig:\n900\t  setting1: value1\n901\t  setting1: value2\n902\t  setting2: value3\n903\t\"#;\n904\t\n905\t  let parser = BasicParser::strict();\n914\t\n915\t        let yaml = r#\"\n916\tsection1:\n917\t  key: value1\n918\tsection2:\n919\t  key: value2\n920\tsection3:\n921\t  key: /// Test real-world config scenario\n929\t    #[test]\n930\t    fn test_real_world_config_scenario() {\n931\t        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n932\t\n933\t        let yaml = r#\"\n934\tapplication:\n935\t  name: myapp\n936\t  version: 1.0.0\n937\t\n938\tserver:\n939\t  host: 0.0.0.0\n940\t  port: 8080\n941\t  ssl:\n942\t    enabled: true\n943\t    cert_path: 5432\n952\t\n953\tlogging:\n954\t  level: info\n955\t  outputs:\n956\t    - type: stdout\n957\t      format: json\n958\t    - type: file\n959\t      format: text\n960\t      path:
