Scope representation data structures\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\\n4\\t//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\\n5\\t//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\\n6\\t//!\\n7\\t//! # Overview\\n8\\t//!\\n9\\t//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\\n10\\t//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\\n11\\t//!\\n12\\t//! ```yaml\\n13\\t//! services:\\n14\\t//!   web:\\n15\\t//!     host: localhost    # \\\"host\\\" in services.web scope\\n16\\t//!     port: 8080\\n17\\t//!   database:\\n18\\t//!     host: db.example.com  # \\\"host\\\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\\n19\\t//!     port: 5432\\n20\\t//! ```\\n21\\t//!\\n22\\t//! The scope representation system tracks:\\n23\\t//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\\n24\\t//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\\n25\\t//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\\n26\\t//!\\n27\\t//! # Architecture\\n28\\t//!\\n29\\t//! ## Scope\\n30\\t//!\\n31\\t//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\\n32\\t//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\\n33\\t//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\\n34\\t//! - Line number where this scope started\\n35\\t//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \\\"web\\\" in \\\"services: {...}\\\")\\n36\\t//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\\n37\\t//!\\n38\\t//! ## ScopeStack\\n39\\t//!\\n40\\t//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\\n41\\t//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\\n42\\t//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\\n43\\t//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\\n44\\t//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\\n45\\t//!\\n46\\t//! # Examples\\n47\\t//!\\n48\\t//! ```\\n49\\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\\n50\\t//!\\n51\\t//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\\n52\\t//!\\n53\\t//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\\n54\\t//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\\\"services\\\".to_string()));\\n55\\t//!\\n56\\t//! // Add keys to current scope\\n57\\t//! stack.add_key(\\\"web\\\", 2).unwrap();\\n58\\t//! stack.add_key(\\\"database\\\", 3).unwrap();\\n59\\t//!\\n60\\t//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\\n61\\t//! let result = stack.add_key(\\\"web\\\", 4);\\n62\\t//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\\n63\\t//! ```\\n64\\t\\n65\\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\\n66\\tuse std::fmt;\\n67\\t\\n68\\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n69\\tuse log::debug as log_debug;\\n70\\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n71\\tuse log::warn as log_warn;\\n72\\t#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n73\\tuse log::trace as log_trace;\\n74\\t\\n75\\t/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\\n76\\t///\\n77\\t/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\\n78\\t/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\\n79\\t/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\\n80\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\n81\\tpub struct Scope {\\n82\\t    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\\n83\\t    pub indent_level: usize,\\n84\\t    /// Keys defined within this scope\\n85\\t    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\\n86\\t    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\\n87\\t    pub start_line: usize,\\n88\\t    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \\\"web\\\" in \\\"services: {...}\\\")\\n89\\t    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\\n90\\t    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\\n91\\t    pub is_flow_style: bool,\\n92\\t    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\\n93\\t    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\\n94\\t    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\\n95\\t    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\\n96\\t}\\n97\\t\\n98\\timpl Scope {\\n99\\t    /// Create a new scope\\n100\\t    ///\\n101\\t    /// # Arguments\\n102\\t    ///\\n103\\t    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\\n104\\t    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\\n105\\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\\n106\\t    ///\\n107\\t    /// # Examples\\n108\\t    ///\\n109\\t    /// ```\\n110\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\\n111\\t    ///\\n112\\t    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\\\"services\\\".to_string()));\\n113\\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\\n114\\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\\n115\\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\\\"services\\\".to_string()));\\n116\\t    /// ```\\n117\\t    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\\n118\\t        Self {\\n119\\t            indent_level,\\n120\\t            keys: HashSet::new(),\\n121\\t            start_line,\\n122\\t            parent_key,\\n123\\t            is_flow_style: false,\\n124\\t            in_sequence_context: false,\\n125\\t            sequence_item_id: None,\\n126\\t        }\\n127\\t    }\\n128\\t\\n129\\t    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\\n130\\t    ///\\n131\\t    /// # Arguments\\n132\\t    ///\\n133\\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\\n134\\t    ///\\n135\\t    /// # Returns\\n136\\t    ///\\n137\\t    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\\n138\\t    ///\\n139\\t    /// # Examples\\n140\\t    ///\\n141\\t    /// ```\\n142\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\\n143\\t    ///\\n144\\t    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\\n145\\t    ///\\n146\\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\\\"first\\\"), false); // First occurrence\\n147\\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\\\"second\\\"), false); // Different key\\n148\\t    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\\\"first\\\"), true);  // Duplicate!\\n149\\t    /// ```\\n150\\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\\n151\\t        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\\n152\\t    }\\n153\\t\\n154\\t    /// Check if this scope contains a key\\n155\\t    ///\\n156\\t    /// # Arguments\\n157\\t    ///\\n158\\t    /// * `key` - The key to check\\n159\\t    ///\\n160\\t    /// # Returns\\n161\\t    ///\\n162\\t    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\\n163\\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\\n164\\t        self.keys.contains(key)\\n165\\t    }\\n166\\t\\n167\\t    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\\n168\\t    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\\n169\\t        self.keys.len()\\n170\\t    }\\n171\\t\\n172\\t    /// Clear all keys from this scope\\n173\\t    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\\n174\\t        self.keys.clear();\\n175\\t    }\\n176\\t}\\n177\\t\\n178\\timpl fmt::Display for Scope {\\n179\\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n180\\t        write!(f, \\\"Scope(indent={}, \\\", self.indent_level)?;\\n181\\t        if let Some(ref parent) = self.parent_key {\\n182\\t            write!(f, \\\"parent={}, \\\", parent)?;\\n183\\t        }\\n184\\t        write!(f, \\\"keys={})\\\", self.keys.len())\\n185\\t    }\\n186\\t}\\n187\\t\\n188\\t/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\\n189\\t///\\n190\\t/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\\n191\\t/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\\n192\\t/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\\n193\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\n194\\tpub struct ScopeStack {\\n195\\t    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\\n196\\t    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\\n197\\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\\n198\\t    base_indent: usize,\\n199\\t    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\\n200\\t    sequence_item_counter: usize,\\n201\\t    /// History of indent transitions (whether or not they have keys)\\n202\\t    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>,\\n203\\t    /// The last recorded indent level (to detect transitions)\\n204\\t    last_indent: usize,\\n205\\t}\\n206\\t\\n207\\timpl ScopeStack {\\n208\\t    /// Create a new scope stack\\n209\\t    ///\\n210\\t    /// # Arguments\\n211\\t    ///\\n212\\t    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\\n213\\t    ///\\n214\\t    /// # Examples\\n215\\t    ///\\n216\\t    /// ```\\n217\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\\n218\\t    ///\\n219\\t    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\\n220\\t    /// ```\\n221\\t    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\\n222\\t        Self {\\n223\\t            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\\n224\\t            base_indent,\\n225\\t            sequence_item_counter: 0,\\n226\\t            indent_transitions: Vec::new(),\\n227\\t            last_indent: 0,\\n228\\t        }\\n229\\t    }\\n230\\t\\n231\\t    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\\n232\\t    ///\\n233\\t    /// # Panics\\n234\\t    ///\\n235\\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\\n236\\t    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\\n237\\t        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\\\"Scope stack should never be empty\\\")\\n238\\t    }\\n239\\t\\n240\\t    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\\n241\\t    ///\\n242\\t    /// # Panics\\n243\\t    ///\\n244\\t    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\\n245\\t    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\\n246\\t        self.scopes.last().expect(\\\"Scope stack should never be empty\\\")\\n247\\t    }\\n248\\t\\n249\\t    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\\n250\\t    ///\\n251\\t    /// # Arguments\\n252\\t    ///\\n253\\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\\n254\\t    ///\\n255\\t    /// # Returns\\n256\\t    ///\\n257\\t    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\\n258\\t    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\\n259\\t        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\\n260\\t    }\\n261\\t\\n262\\t    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\\n263\\t    ///\\n264\\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\\n265\\t    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\\n266\\t    ///\\n267\\t    /// # Arguments\\n268\\t    ///\\n269\\t    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\\n270\\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\\n271\\t    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\\n272\\t    ///\\n273\\t    /// # Examples\\n274\\t    ///\\n275\\t    /// ```\\n276\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\\n277\\t    ///\\n278\\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\\n279\\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\\\"services\\\".to_string()));\\n280\\t    /// ```\\n281\\t    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\\n282\\t        // Debug logging for scope entry\\n283\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n284\\t        {\\n285\\t            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\\\"<anonymous>\\\");\\n286\\t            let scope_type = \\\"Mapping\\\";\\n287\\t            log_debug!(\\n288\\t                \\\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\\\",\\n289\\t                scope_type,\\n290\\t                line,\\n291\\t                indent_level,\\n292\\t                parent_info,\\n293\\t                self.depth(),\\n294\\t                self.get_scope_path()\\n295\\t            );\\n296\\t        }\\n297\\t\\n298\\t        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\\n299\\t        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\\n300\\t            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\\n301\\t            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\\n302\\t            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\\n303\\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\\n304\\t\\n305\\t            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\\n306\\t            let before_depth = self.depth();\\n307\\t            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\\n308\\t\\n309\\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n310\\t            {\\n311\\t                let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\\n312\\t                    .filter(|s| s.indent_level > indent_level)\\n313\\t                    .map(|s| format!(\\\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\\\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\\n314\\t                    .collect();\\n315\\t                if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\\n316\\t                    log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removing {} scopes: {:?}\\\",\\n317\\t                             scopes_to_remove.len(),\\n318\\t                             scopes_to_remove);\\n319\\t                }\\n320\\t            }\\n321\\t\\n322\\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= indent_level);\\n323\\t\\n324\\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n325\\t            {\\n326\\t                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\\n327\\t                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\\n328\\t                if removed_count > 0 {\\n329\\t                    log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Mapping reuse cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\\\",\\n330\\t                             removed_count,\\n331\\t                             before_path,\\n332\\t                             self.get_scope_path());\\n333\\t                }\\n334\\t            }\\n335\\t\\n336\\t            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\\n337\\t\\n338\\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n339\\t            {\\n340\\t                log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\\\", indent_level);\\n341\\t            }\\n342\\t        } else {\\n343\\t            // Create new scope\\n344\\t            let new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\\n345\\t            self.scopes.push(new_scope);\\n346\\t\\n347\\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n348\\t            {\\n349\\t                log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (new), indent={}, created new scope at this level\\\", indent_level);\\n350\\t            }\\n351\\t        }\\n352\\t\\n353\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n354\\t        {\\n355\\t            log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE ENTRY] After entry: depth={}, current_indent={}\\\", self.depth(), self.current_indent());\\n356\\t        }\\n357\\t    }\\n358\\t\\n359\\t    /// Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases)\\n360\\t    ///\\n361\\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters decreased indentation,\\n362\\t    /// indicating exit from a nested scope back to its parent.\\n363\\t    ///\\n364\\t    /// # Arguments\\n365\\t    ///\\n366\\t    /// * `target_indent` - The indentation level to exit to\\n367\\t    ///\\n368\\t    /// # Examples\\n369\\t    ///\\n370\\t    /// ```\\n371\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\\n372\\t    ///\\n373\\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\\n374\\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\\\"parent\\\".to_string()));\\n375\\t    /// stack.exit_to_scope(0); // Exit back to root\\n376\\t    /// ```\\n377\\t    pub fn exit_to_scope(&mut self, target_indent: usize) {\\n378\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n379\\t        {\\n380\\t            log_debug!(\\n381\\t                \\\"[SCOPE EXIT] target_indent={}, current_depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\\\",\\n382\\t                target_indent,\\n383\\t                self.depth(),\\n384\\t                self.current_indent(),\\n385\\t                self.get_scope_path()\\n386\\t            );\\n387\\t        }\\n388\\t\\n389\\t        // Edge case: can't exit to a deeper level than current\\n390\\t        if target_indent > self.current_indent() {\\n391\\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n392\\t            {\\n393\\t                log_warn!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: target_indent={} > current_indent={}, ignoring exit request\\\", target_indent, self.current_indent());\\n394\\t            }\\n395\\t            return;\\n396\\t        }\\n397\\t\\n398\\t        // Edge case: can't exit if stack would become empty\\n399\\t        let would_be_empty = self.scopes.iter()\\n400\\t            .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\\n401\\t            .count() == 0;\\n402\\t\\n403\\t        if would_be_empty {\\n404\\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n405\\t            {\\n406\\t                log_warn!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: exit would empty scope stack, keeping at least root scope\\\");\\n407\\t            }\\n408\\t            // Keep at least the root scope\\n409\\t            self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level == 0);\\n410\\t            return;\\n411\\t        }\\n412\\t\\n413\\t        // Remove all scopes deeper than target\\n414\\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\\n415\\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent);\\n416\\t\\n417\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n418\\t        {\\n419\\t            log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] Removed {} scopes deeper than indent={}\\\", before_depth - self.depth(), target_indent);\\n420\\t        }\\n421\\t\\n422\\t        // Search for target scope in hierarchy\\n423\\t        // This handles cases where the exact target indent doesn't exist in the stack\\n424\\t        // by finding the closest parent scope or creating a fallback if needed\\n425\\t        if !self.scopes.iter().any(|s| s.indent_level == target_indent) {\\n426\\t            // No exact match at target indent - search for closest parent\\n427\\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n428\\t            {\\n429\\t                log_warn!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No scope found at target_indent={}, searching for closest parent scope\\\",\\n430\\t                         target_indent);\\n431\\t            }\\n432\\t\\n433\\t            // Find the closest scope with indent <= target_indent\\n434\\t            let closest_scope = self.scopes.iter()\\n435\\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level <= target_indent)\\n436\\t                .max_by_key(|s| s.indent_level);\\n437\\t\\n438\\t            match closest_scope {\\n439\\t                Some(scope) => {\\n440\\t                    // Found a parent scope - use that instead\\n441\\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n442\\t                    {\\n443\\t                        log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] Found closest parent scope at indent={}, path='{}', using existing scope\\\",\\n444\\t                                 scope.indent_level, self.get_scope_path());\\n445\\t                    }\\n446\\t                    // We've already retained scopes <= target_indent, so we're at the closest scope\\n447\\t                }\\n448\\t                None => {\\n449\\t                    // No suitable parent scope found - this shouldn't happen in normal YAML\\n450\\t                    // but we handle it gracefully by creating a fallback scope\\n451\\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n452\\t                    {\\n453\\t                        log_warn!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] WARNING: No suitable parent scope found, creating fallback scope at indent={}\\\",\\n454\\t                                 target_indent);\\n455\\t                    }\\n456\\t                    let fallback_scope = Scope::new(target_indent, 0, None);\\n457\\t                    self.scopes.push(fallback_scope);\\n458\\t                }\\n459\\t            }\\n460\\t        }\\n461\\t\\n462\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n463\\t        {\\n464\\t            log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] After exit: depth={}, current_indent={}, path='{}'\\\", self.depth(), self.current_indent(), self.get_scope_path());\\n465\\t        }\\n466\\t    }\\n467\\t\\n468\\t    /// Check if current scope contains a key\\n469\\t    ///\\n470\\t    /// # Arguments\\n471\\t    ///\\n472\\t    /// * `key` - The key to check for\\n473\\t    ///\\n474\\t    /// # Returns\\n475\\t    ///\\n476\\t    /// `true` if the key exists in the current scope, `false` otherwise\\n477\\t    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\\n478\\t        self.scopes.last()\\n479\\t            .and_then(|scope| Some(scope.contains_key(key)))\\n480\\t            .unwrap_or(false)\\n481\\t    }\\n482\\t\\n483\\t    /// Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key\\n484\\t    ///\\n485\\t    /// This searches through all scopes in the stack, not just the current one.\\n486\\t    ///\\n487\\t    /// # Arguments\\n488\\t    ///\\n489\\t    /// * `key` - The key to search for\\n490\\t    ///\\n491\\t    /// # Returns\\n492\\t    ///\\n493\\t    /// `true` if the key exists in any scope, `false` otherwise\\n494\\t    pub fn contains_key_in_any_scope(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\\n495\\t        self.scopes.iter().any(|scope| scope.contains_key(key))\\n496\\t    }\\n497\\t\\n498\\t    /// Add a key to current scope\\n499\\t    ///\\n500\\t    /// # Arguments\\n501\\t    ///\\n502\\t    /// * `key` - The key to add\\n503\\t    /// * `line` - The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\\n504\\t    ///\\n505\\t    /// # Returns\\n506\\t    ///\\n507\\t    /// `Ok(())` if the key was added successfully\\n508\\t    /// `Err(DuplicateKeyError)` if the key already exists in the current scope\\n509\\t    ///\\n510\\t    /// # Examples\\n511\\t    ///\\n512\\t    /// ```\\n513\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\\n514\\t    ///\\n515\\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\\n516\\t    ///\\n517\\t    /// // First occurrence succeeds\\n518\\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\\\"host\\\", 1).is_ok());\\n519\\t    ///\\n520\\t    /// // Second occurrence fails\\n521\\t    /// assert!(stack.add_key(\\\"host\\\", 2).is_err());\\n522\\t    /// ```\\n523\\t    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str, line: usize) -> Result<(), DuplicateKeyError> {\\n524\\t        let scope_path = self.get_scope_path();\\n525\\t\\n526\\t        // Check if key exists before adding\\n527\\t        if self.contains_key(key) {\\n528\\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\\n529\\t            Err(DuplicateKeyError {\\n530\\t                key: key.to_string(),\\n531\\t                scope_path,\\n532\\t                first_line: scope.start_line,\\n533\\t                duplicate_line: line,\\n534\\t            })\\n535\\t        } else {\\n536\\t            let scope = self.current_scope();\\n537\\t            scope.add_key(key);\\n538\\t            Ok(())\\n539\\t        }\\n540\\t    }\\n541\\t\\n542\\t    /// Get human-readable path to current scope\\n543\\t    ///\\n544\\t    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\\n545\\t    /// e.g., \\\"services.web.database\\\" for a deeply nested scope.\\n546\\t    ///\\n547\\t    /// # Returns\\n548\\t    ///\\n549\\t    /// A string representing the scope path\\n550\\t    ///\\n551\\t    /// # Examples\\n552\\t    ///\\n553\\t    /// ```\\n554\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\\n555\\t    ///\\n556\\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\\n557\\t    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\\\"services\\\".to_string()));\\n558\\t    /// stack.enter_scope(4, 2, Some(\\\"web\\\".to_string()));\\n559\\t    /// assert_eq!(stack.get_scope_path(), \\\"services.web\\\");\\n560\\t    /// ```\\n561\\t    pub fn get_scope_path(&self) -> String {\\n562\\t        let mut path = Vec::new();\\n563\\t        for scope in &self.scopes {\\n564\\t            if let Some(ref key) = scope.parent_key {\\n565\\t                path.push(key.clone());\\n566\\t            }\\n567\\t        }\\n568\\t        path.join(\\\".\\\")\\n569\\t    }\\n570\\t\\n571\\t    /// Get current indent level\\n572\\t    ///\\n573\\t    /// # Returns\\n574\\t    ///\\n575\\t    /// The indentation level of the current scope\\n576\\t    pub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\\n577\\t        self.scopes.last()\\n578\\t            .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\\n579\\t            .unwrap_or(0)\\n580\\t    }\\n581\\t\\n582\\t    /// Get the number of active scopes in the stack\\n583\\t    pub fn depth(&self) -> usize {\\n584\\t        self.scopes.len()\\n585\\t    }\\n586\\t\\n587\\t    /// Clear all scopes and reset to root\\n588\\t    ///\\n589\\t    /// This resets the scope stack to its initial state with only the root scope.\\n590\\t    pub fn reset(&mut self) {\\n591\\t        self.scopes = vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)];\\n592\\t        self.clear_indent_transitions();\\n593\\t    }\\n594\\t\\n595\\t    /// Get the base indentation size\\n596\\t    pub fn base_indent(&self) -> usize {\\n597\\t        self.base_indent\\n598\\t    }\\n599\\t\\n600\\t    /// Set the base indentation size\\n601\\t    ///\\n602\\t    /// # Arguments\\n603\\t    ///\\n604\\t    /// * `base_indent` - The new base indentation size in spaces\\n605\\t    pub fn set_base_indent(&mut self, base_indent: usize) {\\n606\\t        self.base_indent = base_indent;\\n607\\t    }\\n608\\t\\n609\\t    /// Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item)\\n610\\t    ///\\n611\\t    /// This method is called when the parser encounters a sequence item,\\n612\\t    /// creating a new scope for that item to prevent false duplicate detection\\n613\\t    /// between items at the same indentation level.\\n614\\t    ///\\n615\\t    /// # Arguments\\n616\\t    ///\\n617\\t    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level of the sequence item\\n618\\t    /// * `line` - The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\\n619\\t    ///\\n620\\t    /// # Examples\\n621\\t    ///\\n622\\t    /// ```\\n623\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\\n624\\t    ///\\n625\\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\\n626\\t    /// stack.enter_sequence_scope(2, 5); // Enter sequence item at indent 2\\n627\\t    /// ```\\n628\\t    pub fn enter_sequence_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize) {\\n629\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n630\\t        {\\n631\\t            log_debug!(\\n632\\t                \\\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, line={}, indent={}, current_depth={}, path='{}'\\\",\\n633\\t                line,\\n634\\t                indent_level,\\n635\\t                self.depth(),\\n636\\t                self.get_scope_path()\\n637\\t            );\\n638\\t        }\\n639\\t\\n640\\t        // Remove all scopes at or deeper than this level\\n641\\t        // When entering a sequence scope, we clear any existing scope at this level\\n642\\t        // (including parent mappings) and all deeper scopes, then add a new sequence scope\\n643\\t        let before_depth = self.depth();\\n644\\t        let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\\n645\\t\\n646\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n647\\t        {\\n648\\t            let scopes_to_remove: Vec<_> = self.scopes.iter()\\n649\\t                .filter(|s| s.indent_level >= indent_level)\\n650\\t                .map(|s| format!(\\\"(indent={}, parent={:?})\\\", s.indent_level, s.parent_key))\\n651\\t                .collect();\\n652\\t            if !scopes_to_remove.is_empty() {\\n653\\t                log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removing {} scopes at or deeper than indent={}: {:?}\\\",\\n654\\t                         scopes_to_remove.len(),\\n655\\t                         indent_level,\\n656\\t                         scopes_to_remove);\\n657\\t            }\\n658\\t        }\\n659\\t\\n660\\t        // Only retain scopes shallower than indent_level\\n661\\t        // Sequence scopes completely replace any mapping at the same level\\n662\\t        // (unlike sibling mappings which preserve the parent)\\n663\\t        self.scopes.retain(|s| s.indent_level < indent_level);\\n664\\t\\n665\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n666\\t        {\\n667\\t            let removed_count = before_depth - self.depth();\\n668\\t            if removed_count > 0 {\\n669\\t                log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE EXIT] type=Sequence entry cleanup, removed {} scopes: '{}' -> '{}'\\\",\\n670\\t                         removed_count,\\n671\\t                         before_path,\\n672\\t                         self.get_scope_path());\\n673\\t            }\\n674\\t            log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, indent={}, cleared scopes at or deeper than this level (replaces any parent mapping)\\\", indent_level);\\n675\\t        }\\n676\\t\\n677\\t        // Always create a new scope for each sequence item\\n678\\t        // Each sequence item needs a fresh scope - we don't reuse existing sequence scopes\\n679\\t        let mut new_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, None);\\n680\\t        new_scope.in_sequence_context = true;\\n681\\t        self.sequence_item_counter += 1;\\n682\\t        new_scope.sequence_item_id = Some(self.sequence_item_counter);\\n683\\t        self.scopes.push(new_scope);\\n684\\t\\n685\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n686\\t        {\\n687\\t            log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence (new), item_id={}, created new sequence scope\\\", self.sequence_item_counter);\\n688\\t        }\\n689\\t\\n690\\t        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n691\\t        {\\n692\\t            log_debug!(\\\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Sequence, after entry: depth={}, in_sequence={}\\\", self.depth(), self.in_sequence_context());\\n693\\t        }\\n694\\t    }\\n695\\t\\n696\\t    /// Check if we're in a sequence context\\n697\\t    ///\\n698\\t    /// # Returns\\n699\\t    ///\\n700\\t    /// `true` if the current scope is within a sequence context, `false` otherwise\\n701\\t    pub fn in_sequence_context(&self) -> bool {\\n702\\t        self.scopes.last()\\n703\\t            .map(|scope| scope.in_sequence_context)\\n704\\t            .unwrap_or(false)\\n705\\t    }\\n706\\t\\n707\\t    /// Record an indent transition\\n708\\t    ///\\n709\\t    /// This method tracks indentation level changes during parsing, whether or not\\n710\\t    /// they occur on lines with key tokens. This enables detection of indent changes\\n711\\t    /// on blank lines, comments, or other non-key lines.\\n712\\t    ///\\n713\\t    /// # Arguments\\n714\\t    ///\\n715\\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\\n716\\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\\n717\\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\\n718\\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content (for debugging)\\n719\\t    ///\\n720\\t    /// # Examples\\n721\\t    ///\\n722\\t    /// ```\\n723\\t    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\\n724\\t    ///\\n725\\t    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\\n726\\t    /// stack.record_indent_transition(5, 2, false, \\\"  # blank line with indent\\\");\\n727\\t    /// ```\\n728\\t    pub fn record_indent_transition(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize, has_key: bool, raw_line: &str) {\\n729\\t        // Only record if the indent actually changed\\n730\\t        if new_indent != self.last_indent {\\n731\\t            let transition = IndentTransition::new(\\n732\\t                line_number,\\n733\\t                self.last_indent,\\n734\\t                new_indent,\\n735\\t                has_key,\\n736\\t                raw_line,\\n737\\t            );\\n738\\t            self.indent_transitions.push(transition);\\n739\\t            self.last_indent = new_indent;\\n740\\t\\n741\\t            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n742\\t            {\\n743\\t                let direction = if new_indent > self.last_indent.saturating_sub(1) {\\n744\\t                    \\\"increase\\\"\\n745\\t                } else {\\n746\\t                    \\\"decrease\\\"\\n747\\t                };\\n748\\t                let key_status = if has_key { \\\"with-key\\\" } else { \\\"without-key\\\" };\\n749\\t                log_debug!(\\n750\\t                    \\\"[INDENT TRANSITION] line={}, {}→{}, {}, raw='{}'\\\",\\n751\\t                    line_number,\\n752\\t                    self.last_indent.saturating_sub(1),\\n753\\t                    new_indent,\\n754\\t                    key_status,\\n755\\t                    raw_line.trim()\\n756\\t                );\\n757\\t            }\\n758\\t        }\\n759\\t    }\\n760\\t\\n761\\t    /// Get all recorded indent transitions\\n762\\t    ///\\n763\\t    /// # Returns\\n764\\t    ///\\n765\\t    /// A slice of all indent transitions recorded during parsing\\n766\\t    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&self) -> &[IndentTransition] {\\n767\\t        &self.indent_transitions\\n768\\t    }\\n769\\t\\n770\\t    /// Get indent transitions without keys\\n771\\t    ///\\n772\\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines without key tokens,\\n773\\t    /// such as blank lines or comments.\\n774\\t    ///\\n775\\t    /// # Returns\\n776\\t    ///\\n777\\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred without keys\\n778\\t    pub fn get_transitions_without_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\\n779\\t        self.indent_transitions\\n780\\t            .iter()\\n781\\t            .filter(|t| t.is_without_key())\\n782\\t            .collect()\\n783\\t    }\\n784\\t\\n785\\t    /// Get indent transitions with keys\\n786\\t    ///\\n787\\t    /// Returns only those transitions that occurred on lines with key tokens.\\n788\\t    ///\\n789\\t    /// # Returns\\n790\\t    ///\\n791\\t    /// A vector of indent transitions that occurred with keys\\n792\\t    pub fn get_transitions_with_keys(&self) -> Vec<&IndentTransition> {\\n793\\t        self.indent_transitions\\n794\\t            .iter()\\n795\\t            .filter(|t| t.has_key)\\n796\\t            .collect()\\n797\\t    }\\n798\\t\\n799\\t    /// Clear all indent transitions\\n800\\t    ///\\n801\\t    /// This is typically called when resetting the scope stack for a new document.\\n802\\t    pub fn clear_indent_transitions(&mut self) {\\n803\\t        self.indent_transitions.clear();\\n804\\t        self.last_indent = 0;\\n805\\t    }\\n806\\t\\n807\\t    /// Get the last recorded indent level\\n808\\t    ///\\n809\\t    /// # Arguments\\n822\\t    ///\\n823\\t    /// * `indent` - The indent level to set\\n824\\t    pub fn set_last_indent(&mut self, indent: usize) {\\n825\\t        self.last_indent = indent;\\n826\\t    }\\n827\\t\\n828\\t    /// Process an indent transition without a key token\\n829\\t    ///\\n830\\t    /// This method handles indentation changes that occur on lines without key tokens,\\n831\\t    /// such as blank lines or comments. It determines if the indent change represents\\n832\\t    /// a valid scope transition and triggers the appropriate scope entry/exit.\\n833\\t    ///\\n834\\t    /// # Arguments\\n835\\t    ///\\n836\\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\\n837\\t    /// * `new_indent` - The new indentation level\\n838\\t    ///\\n839\\t    /// # Returns\\n840\\t    ///\\n841\\t    /// `true` if a scope transition occurred, `false` otherwise\\n842\\t    ///\\n843\\t    /// # Examples\\n844\\t    ///\\n845\\t    /// ```\\n846\\t    /// use // Process indent decrease on blank line\\n851\\t    /// let transitioned = stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(5, 0);\\n852\\t    /// assert!(transitioned); // Should exit to root scope\\n853\\t    /// ```\\n854\\t    pub fn process_indent_transition_without_key(&mut self, line_number: usize, new_indent: usize) -> bool {\\n855\\t        use std::cmp::Ordering;\\n856\\t\\n857\\t        // Compare new indent with current scope indent\\n858\\t        let current_indent = self.current_indent();\\n859\\t\\n860\\t        match new_indent.cmp(&current_indent) {\\n861\\t            Ordering::Greater => {\\n862\\t                // Indent increased - but without a key, this is unusual\\n863\\t                // We don't enter a new scope without a parent key\\n864\\t                // Just record the transition for tracking purposes\\n865\\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n866\\t                {\\n867\\t                    log_debug!(\\n868\\t                        \\\"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (increase), NO SCOPE ENTRY (no parent key)\\\",\\n869\\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent\\n870\\t                    );\\n871\\t                }\\n872\\t                false\\n873\\t            }\\n874\\t            Ordering::Less => {\\n875\\t                // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\\n876\\t                // This is valid even without a key (e.g., blank line at end of nested block)\\n877\\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\\n878\\t                {\\n879\\t                    let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\\n880\\t                    log_debug!(\\n881\\t                        \\\"[INDENT WITHOUT KEY] line={}, indent={}→{} (decrease), exiting scope: '{}'\\\",\\n882\\t                        line_number, current_indent, new_indent, before_path\\n883\\t                    );\\n884\\t                }\\n885\\t\\n886\\t                self.exit_to_scope(new_indent);\\n887\\t                true\\n888\\t            }\\n889\\t            Ordering::Equal => {\\n890\\t                // Same indent - no scope change needed\\n891\\t                false\\n892\\t            }\\n893\\t        }\\n894\\t    }\\n895\\t}\\n896\\t\\n897\\timpl fmt::Display for ScopeStack {\\n898\\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n899\\t        write!(f, \\\"ScopeStack(depth={}, base_indent={}, current_path={})\\\",\\n900\\t               self.depth(), self.base_indent, self.get_scope_path())\\n901\\t    }\\n902\\t}\\n903\\t\\n904\\t/// Error when a duplicate key is detected\\n905\\t///\\n906\\t/// This error is returned when attempting to add a key that already exists\\n907\\t/// in the current scope.\\n908\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\n909\\tpub struct DuplicateKeyError {\\n910\\t    /// The duplicate key\\n911\\t    pub key: String,\\n912\\t    /// The scope path where the duplicate was classification\\n964\\t///\\n965\\t/// Enumerates the different contexts in which a key can appear in a YAML document.\\n966\\t/// This classification helps the parser understand how to handle different key types.\\n967\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\\n968\\tpub enum KeyContext {\\n969\\t    /// Key with inline scalar value: \\\"key: value\\\"\\n970\\t    ///\\n971\\t    /// The key \\\"key:\\\\n  - item1\\\"\\n988\\t    ///\\n989\\t    /// The key's value is a sequence of items\\n990\\t    ParentSequence {\\n991\\t        /// The parent key name\\n992\\t        key: String,\\n993\\t    },\\n994\\t}\\n995\\t\\n996\\timpl KeyContext {\\n997\\t    /// Get the key name from any key context variant\\n998\\t    ///\\n999\\t    /// # Returns\\n1000\\t    ///\\n1001\\t KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => key,\\n1006\\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => key,\\n1007\\t        }\\n1008\\t    }\\n1009\\t\\n1010\\t    /// Check if this is a parent key (creates a new scope)\\n1011\\t    ///\\n1012\\t    /// # Returns\\n1013\\t    ///\\n1014\\t    /// `true` if this key creates a new scope (mapping or sequence)\\n1015\\t    pub fn is_parent_key(&self) -> bool {\\n1016\\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. } | KeyContext::ParentSequence { .. })\\n1017\\t    }\\n1018\\t\\n1019\\t    /// Check if this is an inline scalar (does not create a new scope)\\n1020\\t    ///\\n1021\\t    /// # Returns\\n1022\\t    ///\\n1023\\t    /// `true` if this key has an inline scalar value\\n1024\\t    pub fn is_inline_scalar(&self) -> bool {\\n1025\\t        matches!(self, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. })\\n1026\\t    }\\n1027\\t}\\n1028\\t\\n1029\\timpl fmt::Display for KeyContext {\\n1030\\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\\n1031\\t        match self {\\n1032\\t            KeyContext::InlineScalar { key, value } => {\\n1033\\t                write!(f, \\\"InlineScalar(key='{}', value='{}')\\\", key, value)\\n1034\\t            }\\n1035\\t            KeyContext::ParentMapping { key } => {\\n1036\\t                write!(f, \\\"ParentMapping(key='{}')\\\", key)\\n1037\\t            }\\n1038\\t            KeyContext::ParentSequence { key } => {\\n1039\\t                write!(f, \\\"ParentSequence(key='{}')\\\", key)\\n1040\\t            }\\n1041\\t        }\\n1042\\t    }\\n1043\\t}\\n1044\\t\\n1045\\t/// Extract key context from a line\\n1046\\t///\\n1047\\t/// Analyzes a YAML line to determine the context of a key.\\n1048\\t/// Returns `None` if the line doesn't contain a valid key.\\n1049\\t///\\n1050\\t/// # Arguments\\n1051\\t///\\n1052\\t/// * `line` - The YAML line to analyze\\n1053\\t///\\n1054\\t/// # Returns\\n1055\\t///\\n1056\\t/// `Some(KeyContext)` if a key is found, `None` otherwise\\n1057\\t///\\n1058\\t/// # Examples\\n1059\\t///\\n1060\\t/// ```\\n1061\\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{extract_key_context, KeyContext};\\n1062\\t///\\n1063\\t/// // Inline scalar\\n1064\\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\\\"host: localhost\\\").unwrap();\\n1065\\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::InlineScalar { .. }));\\n1066\\t///\\n1067\\t/// // Parent mapping\\n1068\\t/// let ctx = extract_key_context(\\\"services:\\\").unwrap();\\n1069\\t/// assert!(matches!(ctx, KeyContext::ParentMapping { .. }));\\n1070\\t/// ```\\n1071\\tpub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\\n1072\\t    let trimmed = line.trim();\\n1073\\t\\n1074\\t    // Find colon position\\n1075\\t    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\\n1076\\t    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\\n1077\\t    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\\n1078\\t\\n1079\\t    // Skip if key is empty\\n1080\\t    let key = key_part.trim();\\n1081\\t    if key.is_empty() {\\n1082\\t        return None;\\n1083\\t    }\\n1084\\t\\n1085\\t    // Skip if key contains invalid characters (like in flow style)\\n1086\\t    if key.contains('{') || key.contains('}') || key.contains('[') || key.contains(']') {\\n1087\\t        return None;\\n1088\\t    }\\n1089\\t\\n1090\\t    // Strip sequence dash from key if present\\n1091\\t    // Handles: \\\"- name: value\\\" -> key should be \\\"name\\\", not \\\"- name\\\"\\n1092\\t    let actual_key = if key.starts_with(\\\"- \\\") {\\n1093\\t        // Remove the \\\"- \\\" prefix (dash and space)\\n1094\\t        key[2..].trim()\\n1095\\t    } else if key.starts_with('-') && key.len() > 1 {\\n1096\\t        // Dash followed by non-space (invalid, but handle gracefully)\\n1097\\t        &key[1..]\\n1098\\t    } else {\\n1099\\t        key\\n1100\\t    };\\n1101\\t\\n1102\\t    // Skip if actual key is empty after stripping dash\\n1103\\t    if actual_key.is_empty() {\\n1104\\t        return None;\\n1105\\t    }\\n1106\\t\\n1107\\t    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\\n1108\\t    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\\n1109\\t        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: actual_key.to_string() }\\n1110\\t    } else {\\n1111\\t        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\\n1112\\t            key: actual_key.to_string(),\\n1113\\t            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\\n1114\\t        }\\n1115\\t    };\\n1116\\t\\n1117\\t    Some(context)\\n1118\\t}\\n1119\\t\\n1120\\t/// Get leading whitespace length from a line\\n1121\\t///\\n1122\\t/// # Arguments\\n1123\\t///\\n1124\\t/// * `line` - The line to analyze\\n1125\\t///\\n1126\\t/// # Returns\\n1127\\t///\\n1128\\t/// The number of leading whitespace characters\\n1129\\t///\\n1130\\t/// # Examples\\n1131\\t///\\n1132\\t/// ```\\n1133\\t/// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::get_leading_whitespace_length;\\n1134\\t///\\n1135\\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\\\"  key: value\\\"), 2);\\n1136\\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\\\"key: value\\\"), 0);\\n1137\\t/// assert_eq!(get_leading_whitespace_length(\\\"    nested: value\\\"), 4);\\n1138\\t/// ```\\n1139\\tpub fn get_leading_whitespace_length(line: &str) -> usize {\\n1140\\t    line.chars().take_while(|c| c.is_whitespace()).count()\\n1141\\t}\\n1142\\t\\n1143\\t/// Indent transition event recorded during parsing\\n1144\\t///\\n1145\\t/// This struct captures information about indentation level changes that occur\\n1146\\t/// during YAML parsing, whether or not a key token is present.\\n1147\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\\n1148\\tpub struct IndentTransition {\\n1149\\t    /// The line indentation level\\n1154\\t    pub to_indent: usize,\\n1155\\t    /// Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\\n1156\\t    pub has_key: bool,\\n1157\\t    /// The raw line content (for debugging)\\n1158\\t    pub raw_line: String,\\n1159\\t}\\n1160\\t\\n1161\\timpl IndentTransition {\\n1162\\t    /// Create a new indent transition record\\n1163\\t    ///\\n1164\\t    /// # Arguments\\n1165\\t    ///\\n1166\\t    /// * `line_number` - The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\\n1167\\t    /// * `from_indent` - The previous indentation level\\n1168\\t    /// * `to_indent` - The new indentation level\\n1169\\t    /// * `has_key` - Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\\n1170\\t    /// * `raw_line` - The raw line content\\n1171\\t This module provides comprehensive syntax error detection for YAML files.\n4\t//! It detects three main categories of errors:\n5\t//!\n6\t//! 1. **Indentation Errors**: Mixed spaces/tabs, inconsistent indentation levels\n7\t//! 2. **Delimiter Errors**: Missing colons, unbalanced brackets/braces, quote errors\n8\t//! 3. **Structure Errors**: Invalid mappings, malformed sequences, duplicate keys\n9\t//!\n10\t//! ## Architecture\n11\t//!\n12\t//! The detector follows a line-by-line analysis approach:\n13\t//!\n14\t//! - LineTracker: Tracks line numbers and content for error reporting\n15\t//! - IndentationDetector: Analyzes indentation patterns\n16\t//! - DelimiterDetector: Checks delimiter consistency\n17\t//! - StructureDetector: Validates YAML structure rules\n18\t//!\n19\t//! ## Usage\n20\t//!\n21\t//! ```ignore\n22\t//! use armor::parsers::yaml::syntax_detector::{SyntaxDetector, SyntaxError};\n23\t//!\n24\t//! let detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n25\t//! let yaml_content = \"key: value\\n  nested: value\";\n26\t//! let InvalidIndentLevel,\n47\t    /// Indentation increase is too large (> 2 levels at once)\n48\t    ExcessiveIndentIncrease,\n49\t    /// Indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\n50\t    InvalidIndentIncrease,\n51\t    /// Tab character detected (when tabs are not allowed)\n52\t    TabCharacter,\n53\t}\n54\t\n55\timpl IndentationErrorType {\n56\t    /// Get a human-readable large\",\n62\t            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => \"indentation increase is not a multiple of base indent size\",\n63\t            Self::TabCharacter => \"tab character detected in Self::MixedTabsAndSpaces => \"E001\",\n71\t            Self::InvalidIndentLevel => \"E002\",\n72\t            Self::ExcessiveIndentIncrease => \"E003\",\n73\t            Self::InvalidIndentIncrease => Self::TabCharacter,\n86\t        ]\n87\t    }\n88\t}\n89\t\n90\timpl fmt::Display for IndentationErrorType {\n91\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n92\t        write!(f, /// Unclosed brace '{'\n115\t    UnclosedBrace,\n116\t    /// Mismatched quotes (single inside double or vice versa)\n117\t    MismatchedQuotes,\n118\t    /// Unclosed single quote\n119\t    UnclosedSingleQuote,\n120\t    /// Unclosed double quote\n121\t    UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\timpl DelimiterErrorType {\n125\t    /// Get a human-readable description of this error      Self::UnmatchedOpeningBracket,\n162\t            Self::UnmatchedClosingBracket,\n163\t            Self::UnclosedBracket,\n164\t            Self::UnmatchedOpeningBrace,\n165\t Self::UnclosedDoubleQuote,\n170\t        ]\n171\t    }\n172\t}\n173\t\n174\timpl fmt::Display for DelimiterErrorType {\n175\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n176\t        write!(f, \"{}: {}\", self.code(), self.description())\n177\t    }\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t/// Main syntax detector for YAML content\n181\t///\n182\t/// This struct orchestrates all syntax detection checks and provides\n183\t/// a unified interface for error detection.\n184\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n185\tpub struct SyntaxDetector {\n186\t    /// Configuration for detection behavior\n187\t    config: DetectorConfig,\n188\t    /// Track indentation state across lines\n189\t    indentation_state: IndentationState,\n190\t    /// Track delimiter state (brackets, braces, quotes)\n191\t    delimiter_state: DelimiterState,\n192\t    /// Track structure state (keys, sequences, mappings)\n193\t    structure_state: StructureState,\n194\t}\n195\t\n196\t/// Configuration for syntax detection behavior\n197\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n198\tpub struct DetectorConfig {\n199\t    /// Whether to detect tab/space mixing\n200\t    pub detect_mixed_indentation: bool,\n201\t    /// Whether to check for consistent indentation (multiples of base)\n202\t    pub check_consistent_indentation: bool,\n203\t    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4)\n204\t    pub base_indent_size: usize,\n205\t    /// Whether to validate delimiter balancing\n206\t    pub validate_delimiter_balance: bool,\n207\t    /// Whether to detect duplicate keys\n208\t    pub detect_duplicate_keys: bool,\n209\t    /// Whether to detect invalid sequence syntax\n210\t    pub detect_invalid_sequences: bool,\n211\t    /// Whether to detect invalid mapping syntax\n212\t    pub detect_invalid_mappings: bool,\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\timpl Default for DetectorConfig {\n216\t    fn default() -> Self {\n217\t        Self {\n218\t            detect_mixed_indentation: true,\n219\t            check_consistent_indentation: true,\n220\t            base_indent_size: 2,\n221\t            validate_delimiter_balance: true,\n222\t            detect_duplicate_keys: true,\n223\t            detect_invalid_sequences: true,\n224\t            detect_invalid_mappings: true,\n225\t        }\n226\t    }\n227\t}\n228\t\n229\t/// State tracking for indentation analysis\n230\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n231\tstruct IndentationState {\n232\t    /// Whether tabs have been seen in the file\n233\t    has_tabs: bool,\n234\t    /// Whether spaces have been seen in the file\n235\t    has_spaces: bool,\n236\t    /// Previous line's indentation level (in spaces)\n237\t    prev_indent_level: usize,\n238\t    /// Expected indentation levels for nested contexts\n239\t    indent_stack: Vec<usize>,\n240\t    /// Lines with mixed indentation\n241\t    mixed_lines: Vec<usize>,\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t/// State tracking for delimiter analysis\n245\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n246\tstruct DelimiterState {\n247\t    /// Stack of opening brackets/braces\n248\t    bracket_stack: Vec<(char, usize)>, // (bracket_type, line_number)\n249\t    /// Current quote state (None, Single, Double)\n250\t    quote_state: Option<QuoteType>,\n251\t    /// Lines with quote errors\n252\t    quote_errors: Vec<usize>,\n253\t    /// Whether we're inside a multiline block (|, >)\n254\t    in_multiline_block: bool,\n255\t    /// Indentation level of the multiline block start\n256\t    multiline_block_indent: usize,\n257\t    /// Whether we're inside flow-style context (within [] or {})\n258\t    in_flow_context: bool,\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\t/// Quote type for tracking\n262\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n263\tenum QuoteType {\n264\t    Single,\n265\t    Double,\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// State tracking for structure analysis\n269\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n270\tstruct StructureState {\n271\t    /// Stack of nested structures (mapping, sequence, etc.)\n272\t    context_stack: Vec<StructureContext>,\n273\t    /// Hierarchical scope-based key tracking\n274\t    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n275\t    /// Previous line's indentation level\n276\t    prev_indent: usize,\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\timpl Default for StructureState {\n280\t    fn default() -> Self {\n281\t        Self {\n282\t            context_stack: Vec::new(),\n283\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Default base indent of 2 spaces\n284\t            prev_indent: 0,\n285\t        }\n286\t    }\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\t/// Current structure context\n290\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n291\tenum StructureContext {\n292\t    Mapping,\n293\t    Sequence,\n294\t    FlowMapping,\n295\t    FlowSequence,\n296\t}\n297\t\n298\timpl SyntaxDetector {\n299\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with default configuration\n300\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n301\t        Self::with_config(DetectorConfig::default())\n302\t    }\n303\t\n304\t    /// Create a new syntax detector with custom configuration\n305\t    pub fn with_config(config: DetectorConfig) -> Self {\n306\t        Self {\n307\t            config,\n308\t            indentation_state: IndentationState::default(),\n309\t            delimiter_state: DelimiterState::default(),\n310\t            structure_state: StructureState::default(),\n311\t        }\n312\t    }\n313\t\n314\t    /// Detect all syntax errors in YAML content\n315\t    ///\n316\t    /// This is the main entry point for syntax detection. It runs all\n317\t    /// enabled detectors and returns a comprehensive list of errors.\n318\t    ///\n319\t    /// # Arguments\n320\t    /// * `content` - The YAML content to analyze\n321\t    ///\n322\t    /// # Returns\n323\t    /// A vector of validation errors representing all detected syntax issues\n324\t    pub fn detect_errors(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n325\t        let mut errors = Vec::new();\n326\t        let lines: Vec<&str> = content.lines().collect();\n327\t\n328\t        // Reset state for new detection\n329\t        self.reset_state();\n330\t\n331\t        for (line_num, line) in lines.iter().enumerate() {\n332\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1; // Convert to 1-indexed\n333\t\n334\t            // Skip empty lines and comments for most checks\n335\t            let is_meaningful = !line.trim().is_empty() && !line.trim().starts_with('#');\n336\t\n337\t            if is_meaningful {\n338\t                // Run all enabled detectors\n339\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation || self.config.check_consistent_indentation {\n340\t                    self.detect_indentation_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n341\t                }\n342\t\n343\t                if self.config.validate_delimiter_balance {\n344\t                    self.detect_delimiter_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n345\t                }\n346\t\n347\t                if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences || self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n348\t                    self.detect_structure_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n349\t                }\n350\t\n351\t                if self.config.detect_duplicate_keys {\n352\t                    self.detect_duplicate_key_errors(line, line_num_1index, &mut errors);\n353\t                }\n354\t            }\n355\t        }\n356\t\n357\t        // Final validation checks\n358\t        self.finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut errors);\n359\t        self.finalize_structure_checks(&mut errors);\n360\t\n361\t        errors\n362\t    }\n363\t\n364\t    /// Detect syntax errors and convert to ValidationResult\n365\t    pub fn detect_to_validation_result(&mut self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n366\t        let errors = self.detect_errors(content);\n367\t\n368\t        if errors.is_empty() {\n369\t            ValidationResult::success()\n370\t        } else {\n371\t            ValidationResult::failure(errors)\n372\t        }\n373\t    }\n374\t\n375\t    /// Reset all state tracking for a new detection run\n376\t    fn reset_state(&mut self) {\n377\t        self.indentation_state = IndentationState::default();\n378\t        self.delimiter_state = DelimiterState::default();\n379\t        self.structure_state = StructureState::default();\n380\t    }\n381\t\n382\t    /// Detect indentation-related errors\n383\t    fn detect_indentation_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n384\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n385\t\n386\t        // Check for mixed tabs and spaces in leading whitespace\n387\t        if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n388\t            let leading_chars: Vec<char> = line.chars().take(leading_whitespace_len).collect();\n389\t            let has_tabs = leading_chars.contains(&'\\t');\n390\t            let has_spaces = leading_chars.contains(&' ');\n391\t\n392\t            if has_tabs && has_spaces {\n393\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n394\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n395\t                self.indentation_state.mixed_lines.push(line_num);\n396\t\n397\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::MixedTabsAndSpaces;\n398\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n399\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n400\t                    error_type.description()\n401\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n402\t            } else if has_tabs {\n403\t                self.indentation_state.has_tabs = true;\n404\t                // Optionally report tab-only indentation\n405\t                if self.config.detect_mixed_indentation {\n406\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::TabCharacter;\n407\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n408\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n409\t                        format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n410\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n411\t                }\n412\t            } else if has_spaces {\n413\t                self.indentation_state.has_spaces = true;\n414\t            }\n415\t        }\n416\t\n417\t        // Check for consistent indentation levels\n418\t        if self.config.check_consistent_indentation && leading_whitespace_len > 0 {\n419\t            let indent_level = leading_whitespace_len;\n420\t\n421\t            // Check if indentation is a multiple of base_indent_size\n422\t            if indent_level % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n423\t                let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentLevel;\n424\t                errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n425\t                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n426\t                    format!(\"{}: {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n427\t                            error_type.code(), indent_level, self.config.base_indent_size)\n428\t                ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n429\t            }\n430\t\n431\t            // Check if indentation increase is consistent\n432\t            if indent_level > self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n433\t                let increase = indent_level - self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level;\n434\t\n435\t                // Check if increase is too large (more than 2 levels at once)\n436\t                if increase > self.config.base_indent_size * 2 {\n437\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::ExcessiveIndentIncrease;\n438\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n439\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n440\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces exceeds maximum of {} levels\",\n441\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size * 2)\n442\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n443\t                }\n444\t\n445\t                if increase % self.config.base_indent_size != 0 {\n446\t                    let error_type = IndentationErrorType::InvalidIndentIncrease;\n447\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n448\t                        format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n449\t                        format!(\"{}: increase of {} spaces is not a multiple of {}\",\n450\t                                error_type.code(), increase, self.config.base_indent_size)\n451\t                    ).with_line(line_num).with_indentation_error_type(error_type));\n452\t                } else {\n453\t                    // Valid increase - push to stack\n454\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.push(indent_level);\n455\t                }\n456\t            } else if indent_level < self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level {\n457\t                // Decrease in indentation - pop from stack\n458\t                while !self.indentation_state.indent_stack.is_empty()\n459\t                      && *self.indentation_state.indent_stack.last().unwrap() > indent_level {\n460\t                    self.indentation_state.indent_stack.pop();\n461\t                }\n462\t            }\n463\t\n464\t            self.indentation_state.prev_indent_level = indent_level;\n465\t        }\n466\t    }\n467\t\n468\t    /// Detect delimiter-related errors\n469\t    fn detect_delimiter_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n470\t        let leading_whitespace_len = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n471\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n472\t\n473\t        // Check if we're inside a multiline block\n474\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block {\n475\t            // Multiline blocks continue until we find a line with less or equal indentation\n476\t            // that's not empty or a comment\n477\t            if leading_whitespace_len > self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent ||\n478\t               (leading_whitespace_len == self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent && (trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#'))) {\n479\t                return; // Skip lines inside the multiline block\n480\t            } else {\n481\t                // Exit multiline block when indentation decreases\n482\t                self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = false;\n483\t                self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = 0;\n484\t            }\n485\t        }\n486\t\n487\t        // Skip empty lines and comments (but not when inside multiline blocks - handled above)\n488\t        if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n489\t            return;\n490\t        }\n491\t\n492\t        // Handle document markers (---, ...)\n493\t        if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n494\t            return; // Document markers should not be flagged for missing colons\n495\t        }\n496\t\n497\t        // Handle multiline block scalars (|, >, |-, |- , >-, >+, |+)\n498\t        // These appear after a colon, like \"key: |\" or \"key: >\"\n499\t        if trimmed.contains(\": |\") || trimmed.contains(\": >\") ||\n500\t           trimmed.contains(\":|-\") || trimmed.contains(\":>-\") ||\n501\t           trimmed.contains(\":|+\") || trimmed.contains(\":>+\") {\n502\t            self.delimiter_state.in_multiline_block = true;\n503\t            self.delimiter_state.multiline_block_indent = leading_whitespace_len; // Content must be MORE indented than this line\n504\t        }\n505\t\n506\t        // Check for missing colons after keys\n507\t        if !trimmed.starts_with('-') && // Not a sequence item\n508\t           !trimmed.starts_with('?') && // Not an explicit key\n509\t           !trimmed.starts_with(':') && // Not a value\n510\t           !trimmed.starts_with('&') && // Not an anchor\n511\t           !trimmed.starts_with('*') && // Not an alias\n512\t           !trimmed.starts_with('!') && // Not a tag\n513\t           !trimmed.starts_with('|') && // Not a literal block scalar\n514\t           !trimmed.starts_with('>') && // Not a folded block scalar\n515\t           !trimmed.contains('{') && // Not flow style mapping\n516\t           !trimmed.contains('[') && // Not flow style sequence\n517\t           !trimmed.contains(':') && // No colon at all\n518\t           !trimmed.is_empty() &&\n519\t           self.looks_like_key(trimmed) {\n520\t\n521\t            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MissingColon;\n522\t            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n523\t                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n524\t                format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n525\t            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n526\t        }\n527\t\n528\t        // Track bracket/brace balance and flow context\n529\t        for (char_pos, ch) in line.chars().enumerate() {\n530\t            match ch {\n531\t                '[' => {\n532\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('[', line_num));\n533\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n534\t                }\n535\t                ']' => {\n536\t                    if let Some(('[', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n537\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n538\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n539\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n540\t                    } else {\n541\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBracket;\n542\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n543\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n544\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n545\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n546\t                    }\n547\t                }\n548\t                '{' => {\n549\t                    self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.push(('{', line_num));\n550\t                    self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = true;\n551\t                }\n552\t                '}' => {\n553\t                    if let Some(('{', _)) = self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.last() {\n554\t                        self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.pop();\n555\t                        // Update flow context when stack becomes empty\n556\t                        self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context = !self.delimiter_state.bracket_stack.is_empty();\n557\t                    } else {\n558\t                        let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::UnmatchedClosingBrace;\n559\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n560\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n561\t                            format!(\"{}: {}\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n562\t                        ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n563\t                    }\n564\t                }\n565\t                '\\'' => {\n566\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n567\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Single),\n568\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n569\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => {\n570\t                            // Mismatched quote\n571\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n572\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n573\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n574\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (single inside double)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n575\t                            ).with_line(line_num).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type));\n576\t                            self.delimiter_state.quote_errors.push(line_num);\n577\t                        }\n578\t                    }\n579\t                }\n580\t                '\"' => {\n581\t                    match self.delimiter_state.quote_state {\n582\t                        None => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = Some(QuoteType::Double),\n583\t                        Some(QuoteType::Double) => self.delimiter_state.quote_state = None,\n584\t                        Some(QuoteType::Single) => {\n585\t                            // Mismatched quote\n586\t                            let error_type = DelimiterErrorType::MismatchedQuotes;\n587\t                            errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n588\t                                format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n589\t                                format!(\"{}: {} (double inside single)\", error_type.code(), error_type.description())\n590\t }\n593\t                    }\n594\t                }\n595\t                _ => {}\n596\t            }\n597\t        }\n598\t    }\n599\t\n600\t    /// Detect structure-related errors\n601\t    fn detect_structure_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n602\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n603\t\n604\t        // Detect invalid sequence syntax\n605\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_sequences {\n606\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n607\t                // Valid sequence item\n608\t                if self.structure_state.context_stack.is_empty() ||\n609\t                   !matches!(self.structure_state.context_stack.last(), Some(StructureContext::Sequence)) {\n610\t                    self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Sequence);\n611\t                }\n612\t            } else if trimmed.starts_with('-') && trimmed.len() > 1 {\n613\t                // Dash followed by something other than space\n614\t                if let Some(next_char) = trimmed.chars().nth(1) {\n615\t                    if !next_char.is_whitespace() {\n616\t                        errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n617\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num),\n618\t                            \"sequence dash '-' must be followed by whitespace\"\n619\t                        ).with_line(line_num));\n620\t                    }\n621\t                }\n622\t            }\n623\t        }\n624\t\n625\t        // Detect invalid mapping syntax\n626\t        if self.config.detect_invalid_mappings {\n627\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n628\t                let before_colon = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n629\t\n630\t                // Check if colon is properly used after a key\n631\t                if !before_colon.is_empty() && !before_colon.ends_with(' ') {\n632\t                    // Valid key: value pair\n633\t   self.structure_state.context_stack.push(StructureContext::Mapping);\n636\t                    }\n637\t                } else if before_colon.is_empty() {\n638\t                    // Colon at start   self.structure_state.context_stack.push(if trimmed.contains('{') {\n652\t                StructureContext::FlowMapping\n653\t            } else {\n654\t                StructureContext::FlowSequence\n655\t            });\n656\t        }\n657\t    }\n658\t\n659\t    /// Detect duplicate key errors using scope-aware tracking\n660\t    fn detect_duplicate_key_errors(&mut self, line: &str, line_num: usize, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n661\t        // Skip duplicate key detection when inside flow-style contexts ([] or {})\n662\t        // Flow-style YAML uses {key: value} syntax which should not be treated as duplicate keys\n663\t        if self.delimiter_state.in_flow_context {\n664\t            return;\n665\t        }\n666\t\n667\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n668\t        let indent = self.get_leading_whitespace_length(line);\n669\t\n670\t        // Check if this is a sequence item with a key-value pair (e.g., \"- key: value\")\n671\t        // This creates a new scope for each sequence item\n672\t        if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n673\t            if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n674\t                // We have \"- key: value\" format\n675\t                let key_part = &trimmed[2..colon_pos]; // Skip \"- \" to get the key\n676\t                let key = key_part.trim();\n677\t\n678\t                // Only enter sequence scope if it looks like a valid mapping key\n679\t                if !key.is_empty() && !key.starts_with('\\'') && !key.starts_with('\"')\n680\t                    && !key.contains('#') && !key.contains('?') {\n681\t                    // Use the proper scope module to enter a sequence scope for this item\n682\t                    // Each sequence item gets its own scope to prevent false duplicate detection\n683\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num);\n684\t                }\n685\t            }\n686\t            // Sequence items don't need duplicate checking at this level\n687\t            // The keys within the sequence item will be checked when we process them\n688\t            return;\n689\t        }\n690\t\n691\t        // Extract key if this is a key-value pair\n692\t        if let Some(colon_pos) = trimmed.find(':') {\n693\t            let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n694\t\n695\t            // Check if this is a parent key (ends with colon and no value on same line)\n696\t            // A parent key typically has nothing after the colon or just whitespace/comments\n697\t            let after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1..].trim();\n698\t            let is_parent_key = after_colon.is_empty() ||\n699\t                                after_colon.starts_with('#');\n700\t\n701\t            if is_parent_key {\n702\t                // This is a parent key - extract the key name and enter a new scope\n703\t                if !key.starts_with('\"') && !key.contains('#') {\n706\t                        // Enter a new scope for the parent key's nested content\n707\t                        self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_scope(\n708\t                            indent + self.config.base_indent_size,\n709\t                            line_num,\n710\t                            Some(key.to_string())\n711\t                        );\n712\t                    }\n713\t                }\n714\t                return;\n715\t            }\n716\t\n717\t            // Skip if it's a key = key_part.trim();\n723\t\n724\t            // Skip quoted keys and special keys\n725\t            if key.starts_with('\\'') || key.starts_with('\"') || key.contains('#') {\n726\t                return;\n727\t            }\n728\t\n729\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n730\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n731\t            match indent.cmp(&self.structure_state.scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n732\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n733\t                    // Indent increased - this should be a parent key (handled above)\n734\t                    // If we get here, it's a regular key at deeper indent without a parent\n735\t                    // Enter an anonymous scope\n736\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, None);\n737\t                }\n738\t                Ordering::Less => {\n739\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to the appropriate parent scope\n740\t                    self.structure_state.scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n741\t                }\n742\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n743\t                    // Same scope level - continue checking for duplicates\n744\t                }\n745\t            }\n746\t\n747\t            // Check for duplicates in the current scope using the scope module's Result type\n748\t            match self.structure_state.scope_stack.add_key(key, line_num) {\n749\t                Ok(()) => {\n750\t                    // Key was added successfully\n751\t                }\n752\t                Err(dup_error) => {\n753\t                    // Duplicate key detected - add to errors\n754\t                    errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n755\t                        format!(\"key_{}\", key),\n756\t                        dup_error.message()\n757\t                    ).with_line(line_num));\n758\t                }\n759\t            }\n760\t        }\n761\t\n762\t        // Update previous indentation for next comparison\n763\t        self.structure_state.prev_indent = indent;\n764\t    }\n765\t\n766\t    /// Finalize delimiter checks after processing all lines\n767\t    fn finalize_delimiter_checks(&mut self, errors: &mut Vec<ValidationError>) {\n768\t        // Check for unclosed brackets\n769\t        for (bracket, line_num) in ).with_line(0).with_delimiter_error_type(error_type)); // Line 0 since we don't track where it opened\n793\t        }\n794\t    }\n795\t\n796\t    /// Finalize structure checks after processing all  }\n800\t\n801\t    /// Get leading whitespace length from a line\n802\t    fn get_leading_whitespace_length(&self, line: &str) -> usize {\n803\t        line.chars().take_while(|c| }\n813\t\n814\t        let first_char = trimmed.chars().next().unwrap();\n815\t\n816\t        // Exclude lines that start with YAML special characters\n817\t        if matches!(first_char, '-' | ':' |
