Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the Parser trait and provides functionality for\n//! parsing YAML content from various sources.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::{\n    types::{ParseResult, ValidationResult, ValidationError},\n    error::ParseError,\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n    scope::ScopeStack,\n    line_parser::{calculate_indentation},\n    scope::extract_key_context,\n};\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n    ///\n    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n            if indent_changed {\n                // Check if this line has a key context\n                let has_key = extract_key_context(line).is_some();\n                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n            }\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            // However, we DO process indent decreases on blank lines to exit scopes properly.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Process indent transitions without keys (e.g., scope exit on blank line)\n                if indent_changed && !trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                    scope_stack.process_indent_transition_without_key(line_num_1index, indent);\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1424}}}

Scope representation data structures\n//!\n//! This module defines the core data structures for representing hierarchical scopes\n//! in YAML documents. These structures enable accurate duplicate key detection by\n//! tracking keys within their proper scope contexts.\n//!\n//! # Overview\n//!\n//! YAML documents use indentation to define nested scopes. Keys in different scopes\n//! can have the same name without being duplicates. For example:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! services:\n//!   web:\n//!     host: localhost    # \"host\" in services.web scope\n//!     port: 8080\n//!   database:\n//!     host: db.example.com  # \"host\" in services.database scope (NOT a duplicate)\n//!     port: 5432\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The scope representation system tracks:\n//! - **Scope**: A mapping context at a specific nesting level\n//! - **ScopeStack**: A hierarchical stack of active scopes during parsing\n//! - **KeyContext**: Classification of key types (inline scalar, parent mapping, parent sequence)\n//!\n//! # Architecture\n//!\n//! ## Scope\n//!\n//! A `Scope` represents a single mapping context with:\n//! - Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n//! - Set of keys defined within this scope\n//! - Line number where this scope started\n//! - Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n//! - Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n//!\n//! ## ScopeStack\n//!\n//! A `ScopeStack` manages the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes:\n//! - Maintains a stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n//! - Tracks base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n//! - Handles scope transitions (enter/exit) as indentation changes\n//! - Provides duplicate key detection within proper scope contexts\n//!\n//! # Examples\n//!\n//! ```\n//! use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::{Scope, ScopeStack};\n//!\n//! let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // Base indent of 2 spaces\n//!\n//! // Enter a scope when encountering a parent mapping\n//! stack.enter_scope(2, 1, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n//!\n//! // Add keys to current scope\n//! stack.add_key(\"web\", 2).unwrap();\n//! stack.add_key(\"database\", 3).unwrap();\n//!\n//! // Detect duplicate in same scope\n//! let result = stack.add_key(\"web\", 4);\n//! assert!(result.is_err()); // Duplicate key error\n//! ```\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::fmt;\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::warn as log_warn;\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::trace as log_trace;\n\n/// A scope representing a mapping context at a specific nesting level\n///\n/// Scopes are created when the parser encounters a parent mapping (a key whose value\n/// is itself a mapping). Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n/// keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    pub indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n}\n\nimpl Scope {\n    /// Create a new scope\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - Number of leading spaces for this scope\n    /// * `start_line` - Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let scope = Scope::new(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.indent_level, 2);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.start_line, 5);\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.parent_key, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(indent_level: usize, start_line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            indent_level,\n            keys: HashSet::new(),\n            start_line,\n            parent_key,\n            is_flow_style: false,\n            in_sequence_context: false,\n            sequence_item_id: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a key to this scope, returning true if it's a duplicate\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to add\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), `false` otherwise\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(0, 1, None);\n    ///\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), false); // First occurrence\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"second\"), false); // Different key\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.add_key(\"first\"), true);  // Duplicate!\n    /// ```\n    pub fn add_key(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        !self.keys.insert(key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this scope contains a key\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `key` - The key to check\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the key exists in this scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn contains_key(&self, key: &str) -> bool {\n        self.keys.contains(key)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of keys in this scope\n    pub fn key_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.keys.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all keys from this scope\n    pub fn clear_keys(&mut self) {\n        self.keys.clear();\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up scope state before being removed from the stack\n    ///\n    /// This method explicitly clears all scope data to prevent memory leaks\n    /// and ensure no dangling references remain. Called automatically during\n    /// scope exit operations.\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope;\n    ///\n    /// let mut scope = Scope::new(2, 1, Some(\"test\".to_string()));\n    /// scope.add_key(\"key1\");\n    /// scope.cleanup();\n    /// assert_eq!(scope.key_count(), 0);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn cleanup(&mut self) {\n        // Clear all keys to release memory\n        self.keys.clear();\n\n        // Reset sequence context flags\n        self.in_sequence_context = false;\n        self.sequence_item_id = None;\n","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1270}}}

cleaned up\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if the scope has no keys and is in a clean state\n    pub fn is_clean(&self) -> bool {\n        self.keys.is_empty()\n            && self.keys.len())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hierarchical stack of active scopes\n///\n/// The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n/// As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n/// ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n    /// History of indent transitions (whether or not they have keys)\n    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>,\n    /// The last recorded indent level (to detect transitions)\n    last_indent: usize,\n}\n\nimpl ScopeStack {\n    /// Create a new scope stack\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `base_indent` - The base indentation size in spaces (usually 2 or 4)\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let stack = ScopeStack::new(2); // 2-space indentation\n    /// ```\n    pub fn new(base_indent: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            scopes: vec![Scope::new(0, 0, None)], // Root scope\n            base_indent,\n            sequence_item_counter: 0,\n            indent_transitions: Vec::new(),\n            last_indent: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope (top of stack)\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty (should never happen in normal operation)\n    pub fn current_scope(&mut self) -> &mut Scope {\n        self.scopes.last_mut().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope as an immutable reference\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the scope stack is empty\n    pub fn current_scope_ref(&self) -> &Scope {\n        self.scopes.last().expect(\"Scope stack should never be empty\")\n    }\n\n    /// Get scope for a specific indentation level\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The indentation level to search for\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a scope at this level exists, `None` otherwise\n    pub fn get_scope_at_level(&self, indent_level: usize) -> Option<&Scope> {\n        self.scopes.iter().find(|s| s.indent_level == indent_level)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter a new scope (when indent increases)\n    ///\n    /// This method is called when the parser encounters increased indentation,\n    /// indicating entry into a nested scope.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `indent_level` - The new indentation level\n    /// * `line` - The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n    /// * `parent_key` - Optional parent key that created this scope\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// use armor::parsers::yaml::scope::ScopeStack;\n    ///\n    /// let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n    /// stack.enter_scope(2, 5, Some(\"services\".to_string()));\n    /// ```\n    pub fn enter_scope(&mut self, indent_level: usize, line: usize, parent_key: Option<String>) {\n        // Debug logging for scope entry\n        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n        {\n            let parent_info = parent_key.as_ref().map(|k| k.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"<anonymous>\");\n            let scope_type = \"Mapping\";\n            log_debug!(\n                \"[SCOPE ENTRY] type={}, line={}, indent={}, parent='{}', current_depth={}, path='{}'\",\n                scope_type,\n                line,\n                indent_level,\n                parent_info,\n                self.depth(),\n                self.get_scope_path()\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Check if we already have a scope at this level\n        if let Some(existing) = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level) {\n            // We're re-entering a scope level - clear and reuse\n            // This handles sibling mappings correctly\n            let mut fresh_scope = Scope::new(indent_level, line, parent_key);\n            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style;\n\n            // Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n            let before_depth = self.depth();\n            let before_path = self.get_scope_path();\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                let indent_level);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                let after_retain_depth = self.depth();\n                let removed_count = before_depth - after_retain_depth;\n                      before_path,\n                             self.get_scope_path());\n                }\n            }\n\n            self.scopes.push(fresh_scope);\n\n            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n            {\n                log_debug!(\"[SCOPE ENTRY] type=Mapping (reuse), indent={}, cleared scopes deeper than this level\", indent_level);\n            }\n        } Current Indent/Whitespace Handling\n\n**Whitespace Calculation** (`/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/line_parser.rs` lines 761-763):\n```rust\npub fn calculate_indentation(line: &str) -> usize {\n    IndentationInfo::from_line(line).total_level\n}\n```\n\n**IndentationInfo Structure** (lines 527-629):\n```rust\npub struct IndentationInfo {\n    pub leading_spaces: usize,\n    pub leading_tabs: usize,\n    pub total_level: usize,\n}\n```\n\n**Key Indent Handling Features**:\n- Counts both spaces and tabs individually\n- Detects mixed indentation (spaces + tabs)\n- Tracks all indent transitions, even on blank lines\n- Uses base_indent (default: 2) for scope calculations\n\n### 4. Event Emission Patterns\n\nThe parser uses a **conditional scope tracking** pattern rather than traditional event emission:\n\n**Scope Entry Conditions** (`/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/parser.rs` lines 155-204):\n```rust\nmatch indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n    Ordering::Greater => {\n        if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n            if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index);\n                scope_stack.enter_scope(indent + scope_stack.base_indent(), line_num_1index, Some(ctx.key_name()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    // ... other cases\n}\n```\n\n**Indent Transition Recording**:\n- Records all indent changes (with/without keys)\n- Blank lines can trigger scope exits but not entries\n- Comments don't trigger scope changes\n\n**Key Classification System**:\n- `InlineScalar` - \"key: value\" (no scope creation)\n- `ParentMapping` - \"key:\" (creates new scope)\n- `ParentSequence` - \"- key:\" (creates sequence scope)\n\n### 5. Special Line Handling\n\n**Comment Filtering** (`/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/line_parser.rs` lines 1026-1081):\n- Strips inline comments while preserving hashes in URLs/quoted strings\n- Full-line comments are excluded from key detection\n\n**Document Markers**:\n- `---` and `...` trigger scope stack resets\n- YAML directives (lines starting with `%`) are skipped\n\n**Blank Line Handling**:\n- Transparent to scope tracking (no scope entry)\n- Can trigger scope exits on indent decrease\n- Indent changes still recorded for analysis\n\n## Summary\n\nThis parser implements a **line-by-line scope tracking system** rather than traditional token streaming. The \"event emission\" consists of:\n\n1. **Line classification** into types (Blank, Comment, MappingKey, etc.)\n2. **Key context extraction** for mapping/sequence keys\n3. **Scope transitions** based on indentation changes\n4. **Duplicate key detection** within scopes\n5. **Indent transition recording** for debugging/analysis\n\nThe architecture emphasizes scope-aware parsing over token streaming, with comprehensive whitespace handling and indent-based scope management.","usage":{"total_tokens":0,"tool_uses":27,"duration_ms":58603},"uuid":"a07d4f6c-e462-49d7-b67b-9446561bf107","session_id":"829c0e9e-74d9-493e-ab44-821ce8f69316"}

Here's a comprehensive report:\n\n## 1. Parser State Struct Definitions\n\n### Main Parser State: `ScopeStack` (/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/scope.rs)\n\nThe primary parser state structure is the `ScopeStack`:\n\n```rust\npub struct ScopeStack {\n    /// Stack of active scopes (top = current scope)\n    scopes: Vec<Scope>,\n    /// Base indentation size (usually 2 or 4 spaces)\n    base_indent: usize,\n    /// Sequence item counter for generating unique IDs\n    sequence_item_counter: usize,\n    /// History of indent transitions (whether or not they have keys)\n    indent_transitions: Vec<IndentTransition>,\n    /// The last recorded indent level (to detect transitions)\n    last_indent: usize,\n}\n```\n\n### Individual Scope State: `Scope`\n\n```rust\npub struct Scope {\n    /// Indentation level (number of leading spaces)\n    pub indent_level: usize,\n    /// Keys defined within this scope\n    pub keys: HashSet<String>,\n    /// Line number where this scope started (1-indexed)\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    /// Parent key that created this scope (e.g., \"web\" in \"services: {...}\")\n    pub parent_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Whether this scope is in flow-style mapping ({key: value})\n    pub is_flow_style: bool,\n    /// Whether this scope is within a sequence context\n    pub in_sequence_context: bool,\n    /// Unique identifier for sequence items to distinguish them at same indent\n    pub sequence_item_id: Option<usize>,\n}\n```\n\n### Main Parser Structure: `BasicParser`\n\n```rust\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n}\n```\n\n## 2. Current Indent Handling Logic\n\n### Indentation Tracking Mechanisms\n\nThe parser uses multiple approaches to track indentation:\n\n1. **IndentTransition Recording**: All indent changes are recorded whether or not they occur on lines with keys:\n```rust\npub struct IndentTransition {\n    /// The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n    pub line_number: usize,\n    /// The previous indentation level\n    pub from_indent: usize,\n    /// The new indentation level\n    pub to_indent: usize,\n    /// Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n    pub has_key: bool,\n    /// The raw line content (for debugging)\n    pub raw_line: String,\n}\n```\n\n2. **Scope-Based Tracking**: Indentation levels are maintained through the scope stack hierarchy, where each scope has its own `indent_level`.\n\n3. **Current Indent Calculation**: The current indentation is derived from the scope stack:\n```rust\npub fn current_indent(&self) -> usize {\n    self.scopes.last()\n        .map(|scope| scope.indent_level)\n        .unwrap_or(0)\n}\n```\n\n### Indentation Calculation Function\n\nLocated in /home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/line_parser.rs:\n```rust\npub fn calculate_indentation(line: &str) -> usize {\n    IndentationInfo::from_line(line).total_level\n}\n```\n\nThis function counts leading whitespace characters (spaces and tabs equally as 1 level each).\n\n## 3. Where Line Parsing Happens\n\n### Main Parsing Loop\n\nThe primary line-by-line parsing occurs in the `parse_str` method of `BasicParser` (/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/parser.rs):\n\n```rust\nfor (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n    let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n    \n    // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n    if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n        scope_stack.reset();\n        continue;\n    }\n    \n    // Skip YAML directives\n    if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n        continue;\n    }\n    \n    let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n    \n    // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes\n    let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n    if indent_changed {\n        let has_key = extract_key_context(line).is_some();\n        scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n    }\n    \n    // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n    match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n        Ordering::Greater => { /* Enter deeper scope */ }\n        Ordering::Less => { /* Exit to parent scope */ }\n        Ordering::Equal => { /* Same scope level */ }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Line Type Classification\n\nThe parser also includes comprehensive line type classification in `/home/coding/ARMOR/src/parsers/yaml/line_parser.rs`:\n\n```rust\npub enum LineType {\n    Blank,\n    Comment,\n    DocumentStart,\n    DocumentEnd,\n    MappingKey,\n    MappingValue,\n    SequenceItem,\n    FlowMapping,\n    FlowSequence,\n    // ... etc\n}\n```\n\n## 4. Token-Based Scope Detection\n\n### Key Context Extraction\n\nThe primary token detection mechanism is the `extract_key_context` function in scope.rs:\n\n```rust\npub fn extract_key_context(line: &str) -> Option<KeyContext> {\n    let trimmed = line.trim();\n    \n    // Find colon position\n    let colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')?;\n    let key_part = &trimmed[..colon_pos];\n    let after_colon = &trimmed[colon_pos + 1..];\n    \n    // Various validation checks...\n    \n    // Classify based on what comes after the colon\n    let context = if after_colon.trim().is_empty() {\n        KeyContext::ParentMapping { key: actual_key.to_string() }\n    } else {\n        KeyContext::InlineScalar {\n            key: actual_key.to_string(),\n            value: after_colon.trim().to_string(),\n        }\n    };\n    \n    Some(context)\n}\n```\n\n### Key Context Classification\n\n```rust\npub enum KeyContext {\n    /// Key with inline scalar value: \"key: value\"\n    InlineScalar { key: String, value: String },\n    \n    /// Parent key with nested mapping: \"key:\\n  nested: value\"\n    ParentMapping { key: String },\n    \n    /// Key with nested sequence: \"key:\\n  - item1\"\n    ParentSequence { key: String },\n}\n```\n\n### Token Detection Features\n\nThe parser includes sophisticated token detection logic that:\n\n1. **Distinguishes key types**: Identifies inline scalars vs. parent keys\n2. **Handles special cases**: Properly excludes comments, document markers, anchors, aliases, tags\n3. **Tracks sequence contexts**: Special handling for `-` prefixed sequence items\n4. **Manages scope transitions**: Enters/exits scopes based on key context and indentation changes\n5. **Records indent transitions**: Tracks all indent changes regardless of whether they contain keys\n\n## Key Architecture Insights\n\n1. **Hybrid Approach**: The parser combines line-by-line processing with hierarchical scope tracking\n2. **State Persistence**: The `ScopeStack` maintains parsing state across lines, not just within individual lines\n3. **Indent History**: The `indent_transitions` vector provides a complete history of indent changes for analysis\n4. **Token Awareness**: The parser is \"token-aware\" through `extract_key_context` but operates on raw lines rather than token streams\n5. **Scope Isolation**: Each sequence item gets its own isolated scope to prevent false duplicate detection\n\nThis architecture enables sophisticated duplicate key detection that understands YAML's indentation-based scoping rules while maintaining efficient line-by-line processing."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_ee3a1abb595a4349b19b45b5","session_id":"829c0e9e-74d9-493e-ab44-821ce8f69316","uuid":"2ef84b30-f83d-4808-b29a-1b7ab42be40b","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find parser state structure"}

test_tracks_key_presence_in_indent_transitions() {\n153\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n154\t\n155\t    // Start at indent 0, first line has a key\n156\t    // This will record a transition from 0 to 0 (no change) with a key\n157\t    // But actually, record_indent_transition only records when indent changes\n158\t\n159\t    // Let's properly test indent changes:\n160\t    // 1. Start at indent 0\n161\t    // 2. Transition to indent 2 without key (e.g., comment line)\n162\t    stack.record_indent_transition(1, 2, false, \"  # Comment without key\");\n163\t\n164\t    // 3. Transition to indent 2 again with key\n168\t    stack.record_indent_transition(3, 2, true, \"  key3: value3\");\n169\t\n170\t    let transitions = stack.get_indent_transitions();\n171\t\n172\t    // =============================================================================\n437\t\n438\t#[test]\n439\tfn test_scope_stack_records_all_indent_changes() {\n440\t    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n441\t\n442\t    // Simulate parsing a YAML document with various indent changes\n443\t    // The initial last_indent is 0\n444\t\n445\t    // Line 1: indent 0 with key (first line, no transition from initial 0)\n446\t    stack.record_indent_transition(1, 0, true, \"root:\");\n447\t\n448\t    // Line 2: indent 2 without key (transition from 0→2)\n449\t    stack.record_indent_transition(2, 2, false, \"  \");\n450\t\n451\t    // Line 3: indent 2 with key (no transition, same indent)\n452\t    stack.record_indent_transition(3, 2, true, \"  key1: value1\");\n453\t\n454\t    // Line 4: indent 0 without key (transition from 2→0)\n455\t    stack.record_indent_transition(4, 0, false, \"\");\n456\t\n457\t    // Line 5: indent 0 with key (no transition, same indent)\n458\t    stack.record_indent_transition(5, 0, true, \"key2: value2\");\n459\t\n460\t    // Line 6: indent 4 with key (transition from 0→4)\n461\t    stack.record_indent_transition(6, 4, true, \"    nested: value\");\n462\t\n463\t    // Line 7: indent 2 without key (transition from 4→2)\n464\t    stack.record_indent_transition(7, 2, false, \"  \");\n465\t\n466\t    let transitions = stack.get_indent_transitions();\n467\t\n468\t    // We should have recorded the actual indent changes:\n469\t    // - 0→2 (line 2, without key)\n470\t    // - 2→0 (line 4, without key)\n471\t    // - 0→4 (line 6, with key)\n472\t    // - 4→2 (line 7, without key)\n473\t    assert_eq!(transitions.len(), 4, \"Should record actual indent transitions (only when indent changes)\");\n474\t\n475\t    // Verify the types of transitions\n476\t    let with_keys = stack.get_transitions_with_keys();\n477\t    let without_keys = stack.get_transitions_without_keys();\n478\t\n479\t    // Only one transition (0→4 on line 6) has a key\n480\t    assert_eq!(with_keys.len(), 1, \"Should have 1 transition with a key\");\n481\t    // Three transitions without keys (0→2, 2→0, 4→2)\n482\t    assert_eq!(without_keys.len(), 3, \"Should have 3 transitions without keys\");\n483\t\n484\t    // Verify the actual transitions\n485\t Comprehensive tests for indent change detection without key tokens\n//!\n//! These tests verify that the YAML parser correctly detects and tracks\n//! indentation changes even when no key tokens are present on the line.\n//!\n//! Bead: bf-4ccrtq\n//!\n//! Test Coverage:\n//! - Indent changes are detected on blank lines\n//! - Indent changes are detected on comment lines\n//! - Indent changes are detected on lines without keys\n//! - Parser distinguishes indent-with-key from indent-without-key\n//! - Detection doesn't interfere with existing key parsing\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::{ScopeStack};\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::parser::BasicParser;\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::parser::Parser;\n\n// =============================================================================\n// Indent Change Detection on Blank Lines\n// =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_detects_indent_change_on_blank_line() {\n    let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n transition\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_tracks_indent_transitions_across_blank_lines() {\n    let parser = BasicParser::new();\n    let yaml = \"\nroot:\n  level2:\n    level3: value\n\n  level2_b: value2\n\";\n\n    // This should parse successfully, demonstrating that the parser\n    // correctly handles blank lines with indent changes\n    let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n    assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse YAML with blank line indent transition\");\n\n    let value = result.unwrap();\n    assert!(value.get(\"root\").is_some());\n    assert!(value[\"root\"].get(\"level2\").is_some());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_blank_line_indent_decrease() {\n    let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n    let yaml = r#\"\nlevel1:\n  level2:\n    level3: test_multiple_comments_at_different_indents() {\n    let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n    let yaml = r#\"\nroot:\n  # Comment at indent 2\n  level2:\n    # Comment at indent 4\n    key: value\n  # =============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_distinguishes_key_bearing_from_non_key_lines() {\n    let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n    let yaml = r#\"\nkey1: value1\n  # Non-key line at indent 2\nkey2: value2\nkey3: value3\n\"#;\n\n    let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n    assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should distinguish key-bearing from record a transition from 0 to 0 (no change) with a key\n    // But actually, record_indent_transition only records when indent changes\n\n    // Let's properly test indent changes:\n    // 1. Start at indent 0\n    // 2. Transition to indent 2 without key (e.g., comment line)\n    stack.record_indent_transition(1, 2, false, \"  # Comment without key\");\n\n    // 3. Transition back to indent 0 with key\n    stack.record_indent_transition(2, 0, true, \"key2: value2\");\n\n    // 4. Transition to indent 2 again with key\n    stack.record_indent_transition(3, 2, true, \"  key3: value3\");\n\n    let transitions = stack.get_indent_transitions();\n\n    // Should have 3 transitions recorded\n    assert_eq!(transitions.len(), 3, \"Should have 3 indent transitions\");\n\n    // Check first transition (without key, indent 0->2)\n    assert!(!transitions[0].has_key, \"First transition should not have key\");\n    assert_eq!(transitions[0].from_indent, 0);\n    assert_eq!(transitions[0].to_indent, 2);\n\n    // Check second transition (with key, indent 2->0)\n    assert!(transitions[1].has_key, \"Second transition should have key\");\n    assert_eq!(transitions[1].from_indent, 2);\n    assert_eq!(transitions[1].to_indent, 0);\n\n    // Check third transition (with key, indent 0->2)\n    assert!(transitions[2].has_key, \"Third transition should have key\");\n    assert_eq!(transitions[2].from_indent, 0);\n    assert_eq!(transitions[2].to_indent, 2);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_get_transitions_with_keys() {\n    let mut stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n\n    // Record actual indent transitions:\n    // 0 -> 2 (without key, comment)\n
